name: Air Squat
cues: Feet shoulder-width, toes slightly out. Chest tall, knees track toes. Break parallel.

name: Box Squat
cues: Sit back onto box, pause, drive through heels. Teaches hip hinge in squat pattern.

name: Goblet Squat
cues: Hold weight at chest. Elbows inside knees at bottom. Weight through mid-foot.

name: Back Squat
cues: Bar on traps or rear delts. Brace, break parallel, knees out. Drive chest up on the way up.

name: Front Squat
cues: Clean grip or cross-arm. Elbows high throughout. Upright torso is non-negotiable.

name: Leg Press
cues: Feet hip-width. Lower until knees near 90°. Do not lock out at top. Keep low back in contact with pad.

name: Hack Squat
cues: High foot placement loads glutes; low placement loads quads. Full ROM, no slamming the sled.

name: Bulgarian Split Squat
cues: Rear foot elevated. Front shin vertical at bottom. Drive straight up — not forward.

name: Sumo Squat
cues: Wide stance, toes 45°. Weight hangs between legs. Push knees out hard.

name: Pistol Squat
cues: Free leg extended forward. Sit to full depth, heel planted. Use counterweight if needed initially.

name: Glute Bridge
cues: Feet hip-width, heels near glutes. Drive hips to ceiling. Squeeze hard at top.

name: Hip Thrust
cues: Upper back on bench, pad over hips. Full hip extension at top — posterior pelvic tilt.

name: Conventional Deadlift
cues: Bar over mid-foot. Hinge to grip, chest up, lats engaged. Push floor away — don't pull the bar.

name: Romanian Deadlift
cues: Soft knee, hinge back, bar stays close. Hamstring stretch governs depth — not the floor.

name: Sumo Deadlift
cues: Wide stance, toes out. Grip inside legs. Push knees out as you drive hips through.

name: Single-Leg RDL
cues: Soft knee on stance leg. Hinge until torso and free leg are parallel to floor. Hips stay square.

name: Good Morning
cues: Bar on traps. Soft knee, hinge until torso near parallel. Pure hip hinge — not a squat.

name: Kettlebell Swing
cues: Hike the bell back, snap hips forward. Arms are a pendulum — glutes power the swing.

name: Trap Bar Deadlift
cues: Stand in center of bar. More quad involvement than conventional. Safer for lifters with limited hip mobility.

name: Leg Curl
cues: Full ROM — full extension, full contraction. Control the eccentric. Avoid hip flexor compensation.

name: Forward Lunge
cues: Step forward, lower back knee toward floor. Front shin stays vertical. Push back to start.

name: Reverse Lunge
cues: Step back — more knee-friendly than forward lunge. Front foot stays planted. Torso upright.

name: Walking Lunge
cues: Continuous alternating forward lunges. Great for quads and conditioning. Control trunk sway.

name: Lateral Lunge
cues: Step wide, push hips back over bent knee. Straight leg stays straight. Big hip mobility demand.

name: Step-Up
cues: Drive through the heel of the elevated foot. Don't push off the trailing foot. Full hip extension at top.

name: Curtsy Lunge
cues: Step back and across. Targets glute med hard. Keep front knee tracking over toe.

name: Push-Up
cues: Rigid plank position. Elbows 45° from torso. Full ROM — chest to floor, full extension.

name: Incline Push-Up
cues: Hands elevated reduces load. Same technique as push-up. Good regression for those with limited upper body strength.

name: Barbell Bench Press
cues: Arch, retract scapulae, feet flat. Touch chest, drive bar back over rack. No elbow flare.

name: Dumbbell Bench Press
cues: Greater ROM than barbell. Control the dumbbells — don't let them drift wide.

name: Incline Dumbbell Press
cues: 30–45° incline. Targets upper chest / clavicular head. Elbows slightly inside wrists.

name: Incline Barbell Press
cues: 30–45° bench. Bar touches upper chest. Don't let bar drift toward chin.

name: Cable Chest Fly
cues: Cables set high or mid. Slight elbow bend, squeeze at center. Maintain arc — not a row.

name: Dumbbell Fly
cues: Soft elbow throughout. Stretch at bottom, squeeze at top. Load is lighter than press — isolation only.

name: Close-Grip Push-Up
cues: Hands under shoulders or slightly narrower. More tricep demand than standard push-up.

name: Overhead Press
cues: Grip just outside shoulder. Press to lockout. Brace hard — no lumbar extension.

name: Dumbbell Shoulder Press
cues: Seated or standing. Neutral or pronated grip. Control the descent.

name: Arnold Press
cues: Start with palms facing you, rotate out as you press. Hits all three deltoid heads through ROM.

name: Lateral Raise
cues: Slight forward lean. Lead with elbows, not wrists. Pinky slightly high at top. Control down.

name: Pike Push-Up
cues: Hips high, head toward floor. Simulates overhead press pattern. Good shoulder stability builder.

name: Front Raise
cues: Straight arms, raise to shoulder height. Isolation — low load. Don't swing.

name: Inverted Row
cues: Body straight, feet out. Pull chest to bar. Easier than pull-up — great horizontal pull regression.

name: Bent-Over Barbell Row
cues: Hinge 45°+. Bar to lower sternum for upper back; belly button for lats. Elbows drive back — not up.

name: Dumbbell Row
cues: Brace against bench. Elbow drives back and up. Full stretch at bottom — don't short the ROM.

name: Seated Cable Row
cues: Sit tall. Pull to lower sternum. Squeeze scapulae at end. Control the return — don't let cable yank you.

name: Chest-Supported Row
cues: Chest on incline bench eliminates lower back. Full isolation for upper back. Best for beginners or injured backs.

name: T-Bar Row
cues: Hinge position. Use V-handle or wide grip. Big load potential. Keep chest up.

name: Face Pull
cues: Rope attachment at eye height. Pull to face, external rotate at end. Great for shoulder health.

name: Pull-Up
cues: Overhand grip, full hang. Drive elbows down and back. Chest to bar. No kipping.

name: Chin-Up
cues: Underhand grip. More bicep involvement than pull-up. Same full ROM requirement.

name: Lat Pulldown
cues: Lean back slightly. Pull bar to upper chest. Elbows drive down. Stretch fully at top.

name: Single-Arm Pulldown
cues: Unilateral — exposes asymmetry. Pull to hip. Great for lat sweep development.

name: Cable Pullover
cues: High pulley, straight arms. Arc from overhead to hips. Serratus and lat dominant.

name: Farmer's Carry
cues: Pack the shoulders. Walk tall, no torso sway. Grip is the limiter. Go heavy.

name: Suitcase Carry
cues: Unilateral. Resist lateral lean. Anti-lateral-flexion demand. Reveals core weakness fast.

name: Overhead Carry
cues: Lockout overhead, wrist neutral. Massive shoulder stability and core demand. Walk slowly.

name: Bottoms-Up Carry
cues: KB inverted, bell up. Grip and rotator cuff must fire hard to stabilize. Lighter than it looks.

name: Pallof Press
cues: Stand perpendicular to cable. Press out and hold. Anti-rotation — resist the cable's pull. Exhale on press.

name: Cable Woodchop
cues: High to low. Rotate through thoracic spine — not just hips. Straight arms.

name: Cable Reverse Woodchop
cues: Low to high. Drive arms diagonally overhead. Mirror of the woodchop pattern.

name: Russian Twist
cues: Heels off floor, V-sit position. Rotate and touch weight to floor each side. Don't rush.

name: Landmine Rotation
cues: Hold barbell end at chest. Sweep arc from hip to hip. Hip and thoracic rotation together.

name: Plank
cues: Rigid from head to heel. Squeeze glutes and quads. Breathe. Don't let hips sag or pike.

name: Side Plank
cues: Stack feet or stagger. Hips high — don't let them sag. Hand or forearm variant.

name: Dead Bug
cues: Low back pressed to floor throughout. Opposite arm and leg extend slowly. Exhale on extension.

name: Bird Dog
cues: Tabletop position. Extend opposite arm and leg. Hips level — no rotation. Hold briefly at extension.

name: Ab Wheel Rollout
cues: Knees on floor to start. Roll out until nearly parallel, pull back in. Brutal anti-extension demand.

name: Hanging Leg Raise
cues: Dead hang. Control the swing. Raise legs to 90° minimum, toes to bar advanced. Posterior pelvic tilt to engage abs.

name: Cable Crunch
cues: Rope behind head. Crunch — not a hip hinge. Drive elbows to knees. Control the return.

name: Barbell Curl
cues: Elbows fixed at sides. Full ROM — full extension to full contraction. No swinging.

name: Dumbbell Curl
cues: Supinate wrist at top. Elbows stay fixed. Alternating or simultaneous. Control the negative.

name: Hammer Curl
cues: Neutral grip (thumbs up). Hits brachialis — fills out upper arm between bi and tri. Same curl rules apply.

name: Preacher Curl
cues: Upper arm on pad, no cheating. Emphasizes distal bicep. Full extension is critical.

name: Tricep Pushdown
cues: Elbows pinned at sides. Full extension at bottom. Rope or bar attachment. Control the return.

name: Overhead Tricep Extension
cues: Long head is stretched overhead — best way to fully load it. Keep elbows narrow. Lower behind head.

name: Skull Crusher
cues: Bar to forehead or just behind. Elbows fixed, only elbow joint moves. Great for tricep mass.

name: Close-Grip Bench Press
cues: Shoulder-width grip. Elbows stay in. Compound — allows heavier tricep loading than isolation moves.

name: Tricep Dips
cues: Upright torso minimizes chest. Go deep — upper arms parallel to floor. Shoulder health prerequisite.

name: Sled Push
cues: Low angle for speed; upright for strength. Drive through the floor. No stopping mid-set.

name: Battle Ropes
cues: Athletic stance, slight hinge. Alternating waves or slams. Power from hips, not just arms. Stay low.
